replenishment_policy.instruction: EVERY run you MUST add 2-3 new todo tasks to agent_tasks.json, regardless of how many already exist. Read docs/trends.md and search the internet for current AI agent trends before generating tasks. Tasks should be inspired by what top agents (Hermes, OpenClaw, Claude Code, etc.) are doing NOW — not by outdated 2020-era patterns. Never let the pool stagnate. If you cannot search the internet, use docs/trends.md as your source of inspiration.

autonomous_loop_policy.selection_rule: Choose the first todo task that is not blocked by high/critical risk. If the first todo task is too large, create smaller prerequisite tasks and select the first prerequisite.
autonomous_loop_policy.anti_stall_rule: Never ask the user for the next task while safe low/medium-risk tasks can be generated from architecture docs, failing CI, TODO comments, or repeated errors.
autonomous_loop_policy.failure_rule: If CI fails, prioritize a fix task for the failure before starting new features.

tasks.title: Document target cognitive architecture
tasks.description: Create a project-level architecture document that maps biological concepts to engineering contracts and defines the Jules self-improvement governance model.
acceptance: docs/cognitive_architecture.md exists

tasks.title: Validate agent task manifest
tasks.description: Add a stdlib-only validator for agent_tasks.json and tests that prevent malformed tasks, unknown risk levels, missing acceptance criteria and invalid path scopes.
acceptance: python scripts/validate_agent_tasks.py agent_tasks.json
pytest tests/test_agent_tasks.py

tasks.title: Document Jules autonomous operating loop
tasks.description: Define how Jules selects tasks, replenishes the queue, handles failures, avoids clarification stalls and keeps PRs small.

tasks.title: MCPKernel: Taint Tracking and Sandboxed Execution
tasks.description: Inspired by the MCPKernel trend (June 2026), implement an isolated execution environment for code blocks using strict taint tracking to prevent side-channel leaks during execution.
acceptance: MCPKernel runs dynamically generated code securely
Code containing unsafe calls (network, uncontrolled FS) is blocked
Tests mock tainted executions to verify blocking

tasks.title: ASSERT: Policy-driven evaluation framework
tasks.description: Inspired by Microsoft's ASSERT framework trend (June 2026), augment the Metacognition Evaluator to automatically score outputs based on explicitly defined policy documents or rules, rather than just raw heuristic scores.
acceptance: Evaluator accepts a list of policy constraints
LLM evaluates outputs conditionally based on satisfying these constraints
Tests mock the LLM to verify constraint-driven scoring

tasks.title: OpenClaw-RL Online Reinforcement Learning
tasks.description: Inspired by OpenClaw trend: Implement an online reinforcement learning mechanism using MirrorNeurons PAD shifts from user replies to train agent simply by talking. Extract implicit feedback as next-state signals.
acceptance: Agent adjusts policies based on conversation PAD shifts.
Tests verify that positive feedback reinforces recent habits.
Tests verify that negative feedback triggers reflection.

tasks.title: Hermes-inspired Cron Scheduler Expansion
tasks.description: Inspired by Hermes Agent trend: Expand CronScheduler to support daily reports, nightly backups, and persistent scheduled tasks configurable by user.
acceptance: Users can schedule daily reports.
Scheduler persists tasks across reboots.
Tests verify job triggers and serialization.

tasks.title: Export Skills as MCP Tools
tasks.description: Implement a Model Context Protocol (MCP) compatible server interface to expose the agent's internal skills to external clients, aligning with the industry standard for agent-to-tool communication.
acceptance: MCP server can list available skills
MCP server can execute skills via JSON-RPC
Unit tests verify MCP message format compliance

tasks.title: Online RL from User Feedback
tasks.description: Implement online reinforcement learning (OpenClaw-RL pattern) where explicit positive/negative user replies (e.g., 'thanks', 'bad response') are used as next-state signals to adjust tool selection weights in the Basal Ganglia.
acceptance: User feedback modifies action weights
Weights are persisted across sessions
Tests mock feedback and verify weight changes

tasks.title: Agent Guard Policy Layer
tasks.description: Implement an ACS-inspired Agent Guard policy layer that intercepts every tool call with an external effect to run an explicit allow/deny evaluation before execution.
acceptance: All external tool calls pass through Agent Guard
Guard can block calls based on policy rules
Blocked calls return an explanatory error to the agent

tasks.title: AgentBench Evaluation Runner
tasks.description: Inspired by the AgentBench trend from June 2026: Implement a testing harness that allows Magda to be evaluated continuously against multi-domain agent benchmarks, reporting metrics longitudinally to an SQLite database.
acceptance: Evaluation harness can trigger a benchmark suite
Scores are stored in an SQLite metrics DB
Tests verify basic harness functionality using mocks

tasks.title: Cross-Platform Channel Adapter Framework
tasks.description: Inspired by the Cross-platform reach trend: Implement an abstract ChannelAdapter base class and a Discord adapter implementation alongside the existing Telegram bot to allow the agent to reach multiple channels simultaneously.
acceptance: Abstract ChannelAdapter interface exists
A mock or Discord-specific implementation is provided
Messages are successfully routed through the UnifiedMessage pipeline

tasks.title: A2A Task Delegation Protocol
tasks.description: Inspired by A2A Protocol trend (June 2026). Implement an Agent-to-Agent communication module enabling Magda to delegate complex tasks to other specialized agents on the network.
acceptance: A2A delegation client is implemented.
Can send and receive task payloads.
Tests verify successful delegation mocking the external agent.

tasks.title: Context Compression & Selective Retrieval
tasks.description: Inspired by MemGPT/Letta pattern. Implement a context engine hook that aggressively compresses old working memory and uses selective retrieval from episodic memory to keep context window small.
acceptance: Working memory size is continuously monitored.
Compression hook automatically runs when threshold is reached.
Tests verify context is shrunk correctly.

tasks.title: MCP Subagent for Action Tools
tasks.description: Inspired by the Hermes subagent trend. Spawn an isolated sub-agent specifically dedicated to executing complex MCP action tools, ensuring its state and context are completely separate from the main process.
acceptance: Subagent can be spawned explicitly for MCP tools.
Execution context does not bleed into main agent.
Tests verify isolated execution.
